name: ttyd
description: Share your terminal over the web
readme_summary: ttyd is a command-line utility that allows users to share their terminal sessions over the web via WebSockets. It provides a fully-featured terminal experience in a browser, supporting features like file transfer and image output, and can be run on various operating systems.
key_features: Share terminal over the web using WebSockets
Built on libuv and WebGL2 for performance
Full terminal features including CJK and IME support
ZMODEM/trzsz file transfer support
Sixel image output support
SSL support via OpenSSL/Mbed TLS
Basic authentication and custom command execution
Cross-platform compatibility (macOS, Linux, FreeBSD, OpenWrt, Windows)
primary_use_case: Enabling remote access and sharing of terminal sessions through a web browser.
cover_image_prompt: A magical, glowing portal opening from a standard computer monitor, through which a user's command-line terminal session is being projected onto a distant, stylized globe. Inside the portal, digital tendrils of data connect the terminal's input and output. On the globe, abstract representations of users interact with the projected terminal. Subtle elements like a small 'ttyd' logo on the monitor and faint code snippets floating around the portal connect it to technology. The scene is set in a slightly futuristic, clean environment with soft, ambient lighting, rendered in a vibrant 3D isometric illustration style.

name: WAAS
description: Whisper as a Service (GUI and API with queuing for OpenAI Whisper)
readme_summary: Whisper as a Service (WAAS) provides a GUI and API for transcribing audio and video files using OpenAI Whisper. It offers asynchronous job processing, language detection, and various output formats for transcriptions, with an integrated editor for local transcription correction.
key_features: GUI for uploading and transcribing audio/video
API for asynchronous transcription jobs
Queuing system for transcription processing
Email notifications with download links for transcriptions
Local browser-based editor for correcting transcriptions
Support for multiple output formats (JSON, SRT, TXT, VTT)
Language detection endpoint
Webhook support for job status updates
primary_use_case: To provide an accessible and scalable service for transcribing audio and video content, with both a user-friendly interface and a robust API for integration.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, friendly robot with a microphone headset is actively listening to a swirling vortex of sound waves. The robot is efficiently sorting these sound waves into neat, organized stacks of text documents and digital files labeled 'SRT', 'TXT', and 'JSON'. In the background, a subtle digital interface displays a progress bar and a job queue. The overall scene is bright and clean, with a touch of futuristic technology, emphasizing the transformation of raw audio into structured text data. The art style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a vibrant color palette.

name: once
description: Easy self-hosting for Docker-based web apps
readme_summary: ONCE is a platform designed to simplify the self-hosting of Docker-based web applications. It provides an easy-to-use interface for installation, automatic updates, backups, and system monitoring, catering to both TUI and CLI users.
key_features: Easy installation of Docker-based web apps
Automatic updates
Automatic backups
System information dashboard
TUI and CLI interfaces
Support for various hardware (servers, Raspberry Pi, laptops)
Ability to install built-in 37signals apps or any compatible Docker image
primary_use_case: Simplifying the process of self-hosting and managing Dockerized web applications.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, anthropomorphic toolbox labeled 'ONCE' standing on a workbench. From its open compartments, various pre-packaged web application icons (like a blog, a forum, a chat bubble) are neatly emerging and plugging themselves into a simplified server rack in the background. The toolbox is emitting a soft, organized glow, and small, clean UI elements showing status indicators (like green checkmarks and spinning update icons) are subtly floating around it. The scene is bright and clean, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a focus on clarity and ease of use, conveying effortless application deployment and management.

name: Paper2All
description: Code base of 'PAPER2WEB: LET’S MAKE YOUR PAPER ALIVE!' integrating the other work package throughout the entire  "paper2present"  process.
readme_summary: Paper2Web is an autonomous pipeline that transforms academic papers into interactive, explorable homepages. It refines both content and layout to create engaging websites that effectively disseminate research, addressing limitations of current conversion methods.
key_features: Autonomous pipeline for converting scientific papers to homepages
Iterative refinement of content and layout
Creation of engaging and interactive websites
Addresses limitations of direct generation, templates, and HTML conversion
Includes a dataset and benchmark for evaluation and improvement
primary_use_case: To automatically generate interactive academic homepages from research papers.
cover_image_prompt: A magical, open book transforming into a vibrant, interactive website displayed on a floating screen. The book's pages are filled with scientific diagrams and text, which are elegantly flowing as digital streams towards the screen. A friendly, stylized robot agent is shown carefully guiding this transformation, with subtle code snippets and data visualizations appearing around it. The background is a clean, modern digital space with soft, glowing accents. The overall mood is innovative and accessible, conveying the idea of bringing academic research to life online. 3D isometric illustration style with clear iconography and a bright, inviting color palette.

name: argos-translate
description: Open-source offline translation library written in Python
readme_summary: Argos Translate is an open-source, offline translation library written in Python. It leverages OpenNMT for its translation engine and supports installation of language model packages. It also features automatic pivoting through intermediate languages for unsupported direct translations, and is the engine behind LibreTranslate.
key_features: Offline translation
Python library
Command-line interface
GUI application
Installable language model packages (.argosmodel)
Automatic language pivoting
OpenNMT backend
LibreTranslate integration
primary_use_case: Providing an offline, programmatic translation service through a Python library and command-line tools, with support for installing various language models.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old owl, representing Argos Translate, sitting in a cozy library filled with books in various languages. The owl is actively translating a scroll from one language (e.g., Japanese characters) into another (e.g., English letters) using a glowing, digital quill. Around the owl, small, stylized speech bubbles show successful translations appearing instantly. In the background, a subtle, abstract network of interconnected nodes hints at the underlying machine learning models. The scene is rendered in a warm, inviting digital painting style with clear outlines, emphasizing the magic of understanding across languages.

name: next-ai-draw-io
description: A next.js web application that integrates AI capabilities with draw.io diagrams. This app allows you to create, modify, and enhance diagrams through natural language commands and AI-assisted visualization.
readme_summary: Next AI Draw.io is a Next.js web application that leverages AI to enhance diagram creation and modification. It allows users to generate and edit diagrams using natural language commands, integrating with the draw.io editor for a seamless workflow.
key_features: AI-powered diagram generation from natural language prompts
Integration with draw.io for editing and visualization
Support for various cloud provider icons (AWS, GCP, Azure)
Live demo available online
Desktop application and Docker support
Multi-provider support for AI models
primary_use_case: To enable users to create, modify, and visualize diagrams quickly and intuitively using AI and natural language, streamlining the process of technical diagramming.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, anthropomorphic AI assistant with glowing circuits, sitting at a desk and holding a magic wand. The wand is projecting a complex, colorful architectural diagram onto a large, floating canvas. Below the canvas, a user is typing a simple text prompt into a sleek, minimalist interface labeled 'Next AI Draw.io'. The background is a soft, abstract digital landscape with subtle code snippets and data streams flowing around. The overall mood is creative and empowering, showcasing the effortless transformation of text into visual diagrams. 3D isometric illustration style, vibrant colors, clean lines, high detail.
